page: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores using the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor operating at 2.4 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 22,500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7i.24xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and top-tier execution speeds. Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations, rank in the top 10% tier. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores also reach the top 10% tier. Memory performance is robust, with read-only small block cached bandwidth and large block uncached write bandwidth both placing in the top 25% tier. Database operations, evaluated via Passmark and Redis SET operations, show top-tier and strong performance respectively, aligning with the storage-optimized design.

While multi-core and database-oriented benchmarks are highly competitive, single-core CPU performance on stress-ng falls within the average middle 50% tier. The instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware, meaning graphical or highly parallelized neural network training workloads must rely on CPU-based execution. However, the presence of Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets supports accelerated vector and matrix operations, as evidenced by strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference benchmarks. The high density of local NVMe storage and DDR5 memory relative to the 96 vCPUs provides a cost-effective consolidation platform for database workloads that would otherwise require multiple smaller nodes.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core processing, database operations, and software compilation, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While single-core performance is average and the instance lacks dedicated GPUs, its support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions enables strong performance in vector operations and LLM prompt processing. This instance is designed for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive workloads requiring extensive local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe SSD capacity, high-speed DDR5 memory, and top-tier multi-core processing for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in database operations, software compilation, and multi-core benchmarks
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive applications
